Céline Dion announces comeback
shows: 'I'm so ready for this'
7 days ago
Share Save Add as preferred on Google
Mark Savage
Music correspondent
0:46
'I can't wait to see you again': Céline Dion announces comeback Paris shows
Céline Dion has announced her return to the stage, four years after she
was diagnosed with an incurable condition that affected her singing voice
and her ability to walk.
sentences.subject: Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Live Documentaries Céline Dion
sentences.obj: shows Share Save Google Mark Savage Music Céline Céline the an incurable singing

sentences.original:
The star, known for power ballads like My Heart Will Go On and Because
You Loved Me, will play a 10-night residency at the 40,000-capacity Paris
La Défense Arena in September and October.
sentences.subject: The star power

sentences.original: The announcement came on the day of her 58th birthday.
sentences.subject: The announcement

sentences.original:
In a message on
her Instagram page, Dion called her comeback "the best gift of my life".
sentences.subject: Dion

sentences.original: "I'm so ready to do this," she told fans.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original:
"I'm feeling good, I'm strong, I'm
feeling excited, obviously, [and] of course, a little nervous.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
"
Addressing her medical condition, Dion said: "I'm doing great, managing
my health, feeling good.
sentences.subject: Dion

sentences.original:
I'm singing again, even doing a little bit of
dancing.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
"But I have to tell you something very important: Over these last few
years, every day that's gone by, I felt your prayers and support, your
kindness and love.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original: She continued: "I am grateful to all of you.
sentences.subject: She

sentences.original: I can't wait to see you again.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
"
Getty Images
Céline Dion is the biggest-selling Canadian artist of all time
High demand for tickets
Dion, who is one of the best-selling artists of all time, has not performed
her own show since Newark, New Jersey on 8 March, 2020.
sentences.subject: Getty Images Céline Dion
sentences.obj: the selling Canadian artist all time High tickets the selling all

sentences.original:
Her Courage tour was subsequently cut short by the outbreak of the
Covid pandemic, before she was diagnosed with Stiff Person Syndrome
and forced to cancel all future shows.
sentences.subject: Courage tour

sentences.original:
For her live return, all the concert dates are separated by several days,
presumably to avoid undue strain on her physical health.
sentences.subject: the concert dates

sentences.original: Tickets will go on sale from 7 April.
sentences.subject: Tickets

sentences.original:
Demand is expected to be
astronomical, but fans can register interest on her official website from
Tuesday, 31 March.
sentences.subject: Demand

sentences.original:
EPA
Posters teasing the star's comeback appeared around Paris last week
Plans for the comeback were initially leaked by the French-Canadian
newspaper La Presse last week.
sentences.subject: EPA Posters

sentences.original:
Soon afterwards, fans spotted posters featuring Dion's lyrics popping up
around the French capital.
sentences.subject: fans

sentences.original:
On Monday night, shortly after 8pm GMT, the Eiffel Tower lit up with the
message, "Paris, je suis prête" (Paris, I am ready), and fans were treated to
a playlist of songs including I'm Alive, Encore Un Soir and My Heart Will
Go On.
sentences.subject: the Eiffel Tower

sentences.original:
Dion also recorded a French version of the video message announcing the
tour, which was played over loudspeakers as the Tower was illuminated
by purple lights.
sentences.subject: Dion
sentences.obj: a French version the video the the purple

sentences.original:
Difficulty walking
Dion announced she had been diagnosed with Stiff Person Syndrome
(SPS) in December 2022.
sentences.subject: Difficulty

sentences.original:
In an emotional Instagram video, she told fans the condition had affected
"every aspect of my daily life".
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original:
Affecting an estimated 8,000 people worldwide, SPS is a neurological
disease caused by the signals from the nerves to the muscles not working
properly.
sentences.subject: SPS
sentences.obj: a neurological disease the the the

sentences.original: The condition causes muscle spasms and can affect mobility.
sentences.subject: The condition

sentences.original:
For some, it
can be debilitating.
sentences.subject: it

sentences.original: There is no known cure.

Speaking to the BBC in 2024, Dion said she had first noticed the
symptoms when her voice played up on tour.
sentences.subject: Dion

sentences.original: "It was just feeling a little strange, like a little spasm," she said.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original: "My voice was struggling, I was starting to push a little bit.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
"
At the time, she felt it was impossible to take time off, and experimented
with singing in a lower register to ease the stress on her vocal cords.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original: "These shows were sold out for a year and a half, going around the globe.
sentences.subject: These shows

sentences.original: "And I'm going to say to people, 'Excuse me about my spasm?
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
Excuse me
about my je ne sais quoi?'"
However, the condition worsened, "sometimes causing difficulties when I
walk and not allowing me to use my vocal cords to sing the way I'm used
to," she told NBC News.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original: "It's like somebody is strangling you.
sentences.subject: It

sentences.original:
It's like somebody is pushing your
larynx [or] pharynx."
Getty Images
The singer is known for hits like The Power Of Love, Think Twice and Pour Que Tu m'aimes Encore
However, Dion was determined not to let SPS rule her life.
sentences.subject: It

sentences.original:
In 2024, she told French Vogue magazine about the intense efforts she
had made to combat the disease.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original: "The way I see it, I have two choices.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
Either I train like an athlete and
work super hard, or I switch off and it's over," she said.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original:
"I've chosen to work with all my body and soul, from head to toe, with a
medical team.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original: "Five days a week I undergo athletic, physical and vocal therapy.

I work
on my toes, my knees, my calves, my fingers, my singing, my voice.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original: The efforts paid off.

Dion made an emotional comeback at the 2024 Paris
Olympics, singing Edith Piaf's classic Hymne à l'Amour from the Eiffel
Tower.
sentences.subject: Dion

sentences.original:
That was also the first song played as her comeback shows were
announced at the Eiffel Tower.
sentences.subject: That
sentences.obj: the first song comeback the Eiffel

sentences.original: The Canadian star has long had an affinity for the city.
sentences.subject: The Canadian star

sentences.original: "I want to love more when I'm in Paris," she told Vogue in 2024.
sentences.subject: she

sentences.original:
"It makes
me love things more.
sentences.subject: It

sentences.original:
"
